more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe legalises ban on second-hand clothes imports
more.description: Ministry of Industry and Commerce has moved to formalise the ban on second-hand clothing imports, despite concerns over the country’s struggling clothing value chain. The ban, now legally enforced under Statutory Instrument 59 of 2026, prohibits the importation of second-hand clothes unless expressly authorised through a government-issued permit. Under the new regulations, permits will be [...] The post Zimbabwe legalises ban on second-hand clothes imports appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ZCTU backs nurses’ strike, urges govt to urgently address grievances
more.description: The Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) has urged striking nurses to press ahead with their demands for salary increments until the government addresses their grievances. Nurses on Thursday announced that they will down tools from April 15 to April 17, citing poor remuneration and deteriorating working conditions. They said authorities had been given ample [...] The post ZCTU backs nurses’ strike, urges govt to urgently address grievances appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Government extends ZIMSEC examination fees payment deadline to May 22
more.description: THE government has extended the registration deadline for Grade Seven, ‘O’ and ‘A’ level Zimbabwe Schools Examination Council (ZIMSEC) from March 27 to May 22, 2026 after parents requested an extension after failing to raise the required fees on time. The new deadline was announced by Primary and Secondary Education Minister Torerayi Moyo during a [...] The post Government extends ZIMSEC examination fees payment deadline to May 22 appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Eleven nurses arrested, fined over protest at Marondera Hospital
more.description: Eleven nurses were arrested at Marondera Provincial Hospital on Wednesday and each fined US$30 for public nuisance after allegedly attempting to stage a demonstration over poor working conditions and low salaries, in a move that has heightened tensions in Zimbabwe’s health sector. The nurses, who had gathered on March 25, 2026, to air their grievances, [...] The post Eleven nurses arrested, fined over protest at Marondera Hospital appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: French regime cancells South African President invitation for G7 Summit
more.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has been disinvited by France to the G7 summit in the French town of Evian in June because of US pressure, the South African presidency told AFP Thursday. “We’ve learnt that due to sustained pressure, France has had to withdraw its invitation to South Africa to attend the G7 meeting,” […]The post French regime cancells South African President invitation for G7 Summit appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Second-hand clothes imports ban legislated in Zimbabwe
more.description: The Government has officially tightened import controls after gazetting new regulations banning second-hand clothing, except in strictly limited cases where importers obtain permits for charitable purposes. The measures, now backed by law under Statutory Instrument 59 of 2026, mark a significant escalation from previous policy pronouncements, giving authorities full legal authority to enforce the restrictions. […]The post Second-hand clothes imports ban legislated in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: It Is Not Illegal To Hold Millions In Cash In Zimbabwe: Mthuli Ncube Tells Parliament
more.description: It Is Not Illegal To Have Large Amounts Of Cash In Zimbabwe: Mthuli Ncube Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube has told Parliament that individuals in Zimbabwe can legally hold any amount of cash without limitations, meaning people are allowed to hold millions of United States dollars without breaking the law. Ncube made the clarification during [...] The post It Is Not Illegal To Hold Millions In Cash In Zimbabwe: Mthuli Ncube Tells Parliament appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: Unanswered questions after inmate found dead at Chikurubi Prison
more.description: An inmate at Chikurubi Farm Prison, a unit within Zimbabwe’s largest correctional facility, Chikurubi Maximum Security Prison, has died under unclear circumstances, prompting an independent investigation amid social media claims that he may have been assaulted.
